title: Adiantum comoroense
intro: Adiantum comoroense, also known as the Comoro Maidenhair fern, is a species of plant in the Adiantum genus, characterized by its delicate fronds and terrestrial growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Adiantum comoroense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as decoctions and poultices.
